title: Cpk to PPM Converter
description: Converts Process Capability Index (Cpk) to Parts Per Million (PPM) defect rate and provides actionable insights for process improvement. Based on Six Sigma and ISO 22514 standards.

inputs.label: Process Capability Index (Cpk)
inputs.businessContext: Cpk is a standard Six Sigma metric indicating how well a process meets specification limits, accounting for centering. Typical targets: 1.33 (minimum acceptable), 1.67 (good), 2.0 (excellent).

inputs.label: Sigma Shift (long-term drift)
inputs.businessContext: Accounts for long-term process drift. Standard Six Sigma assumption is 1.5 sigma shift. Adjust based on historical data or industry norms (e.g., automotive often uses 1.5).

inputs.label: Distribution Type
inputs.businessContext: Select distribution based on sample size and data characteristics. Normal for large samples (n>30), t-distribution for small samples (n<30) to account for uncertainty.

inputs.label: Sample Size (n)
inputs.businessContext: Number of observations used to estimate Cpk. Larger samples improve confidence. Required for t-distribution adjustment.

inputs.label: Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level for the confidence interval of the PPM estimate. Higher levels provide more conservative (wider) intervals.

inputs.label: Use Confidence Interval
inputs.businessContext: If enabled, the tool calculates lower and upper bounds for PPM based on the confidence level. Useful for risk assessment.

rules.message: Cpk values above 3.0 are extremely rare; please verify input.
Sigma shift must be between 0 and 2.0.
Sample size must be at least 2 for t-distribution.
Confidence level must be between 90% and 99% when using confidence interval.

thresholds.message: Cpk below 1.33 indicates process is not capable. Immediate improvement required.
Cpk between 1.33 and 1.67 is acceptable but consider improvement for higher sigma levels.
PPM exceeds 1000; process is producing excessive defects.
PPM between 100 and 1000; monitor process closely.

formulas.name: Short-term Z-score
formulas.description: Converts Cpk to short-term Z-score assuming centered process.

formulas.name: Long-term Z-score
formulas.description: Adjusts short-term Z-score for long-term sigma shift.

formulas.name: PPM Estimate (Normal)
formulas.description: Calculates PPM defect rate using standard normal cumulative distribution function (CDF).

formulas.name: PPM Adjustment for t-distribution
formulas.description: Adjusts PPM estimate using t-distribution with n-1 degrees of freedom for small samples.
formulas.formula: if distributionType == 't-distribution' then ppm_adjusted = (1 - tcdf(z_long, sampleSize - 1)) * 1e6 else ppm_adjusted = ppm_estimate

formulas.name: Confidence Interval for PPM
formulas.description: Calculates lower and upper bounds for PPM based on confidence level using chi-square distribution for variance.
formulas.formula: if useConfidenceInterval then { lowerPPM = ppm_adjusted * (1 - z_alpha/2 * sqrt(1/sampleSize)), upperPPM = ppm_adjusted * (1 + z_alpha/2 * sqrt(1/sampleSize)) } else { lowerPPM = ppm_adjusted, upperPPM = ppm_adjusted }

formulas.name: Data Confidence Adjusted PPM
formulas.description: Adjusts PPM estimate based on sample size and confidence level to reflect data reliability.

formulas.name: Primary Result (PPM)
formulas.description: Final PPM value used as the primary output. Uses the upper bound if confidence interval is enabled, else the adjusted estimate.
formulas.formula: primaryResult = if useConfidenceInterval then upperPPM else ppm_adjusted

primary.label: Estimated PPM Defect Rate
primary.description: Primary result: estimated number of defective parts per million opportunities.

ppm_adjusted.label: PPM Adjusted (Distribution)
ppm_adjusted.description: PPM after t-distribution adjustment if applicable.

lowerPPM.label: Lower Confidence Bound
lowerPPM.description: Lower bound of PPM confidence interval.

upperPPM.label: Upper Confidence Bound
upperPPM.description: Upper bound of PPM confidence interval.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Low Cpk
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Cpk below 1.33 indicates excessive variability or poor centering. Root causes: machine wear, operator error, material variation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: High Sigma Shift
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Sigma shift > 1.5 suggests significant long-term drift. Possible causes: temperature changes, tool degradation, batch-to-batch variation.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Small Sample Size
hiddenLossDrivers.description: Sample size < 30 reduces confidence in estimate. Consider increasing sample size for more reliable results.

hiddenLossDrivers.label: Non-Normal Distribution
hiddenLossDrivers.description: If actual data is non-normal, PPM estimates may be inaccurate. Consider using distribution fitting or transformation.

suggestedActions.action: Implement process improvement (e.g., DOE, SMED, Poka-Yoke) to increase Cpk to at least 1.67.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Reduces PPM by 50-90% depending on current level.

suggestedActions.action: Stabilize process to reduce long-term drift. Use SPC control charts and preventive maintenance.
Increase sample size to at least 30 for more accurate PPM estimates.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Improves confidence interval width by up to 50%.

suggestedActions.action: Perform normality test (e.g., Anderson-Darling) on actual data. If non-normal, use appropriate transformation or non-parametric methods.
suggestedActions.expectedImpact: Avoids misestimation of PPM by up to 100%.

dataConfidenceAdjusted.label: Data Confidence Adjusted PPM
dataConfidenceAdjusted.description: PPM value adjusted for sample size and confidence level to reflect data reliability.
